ops.title: F6 cregistry tombstone hygiene — both specified fixes are convergence-unsafe
ops.body:
Investigation of F6 (cregistry identity/tombstone hygiene) found the two pre-approved fixes are UNSAFE against verified load-bearing convergence behavior. Codex (gpt-5.6-sol) confirmed the CRDT reasoning.

## Key finding: Remove-on-never-Added is load-bearing, not a bug
`cregistry.Remove(id, at)` on an id with no local entry creates `Entry{Added:0, Removed:at, Value:zero}`. This is the CROSS-HOST DELETE primitive: a host can delete an id that lives on ANOTHER host (not in its own registry) and the delete propagates via `Merge` (per-field max). Structurally identical to the "phantom".

Proven load-bearing by:
- synckit/converge/converge_test.go:158 (TestPullMergeConvergesAndPersists): peer2.Remove("A",40) where peer2 never added A; test asserts A converges to absent after merge with local (A added@10). If Remove no-op'd, A resurrects and the test fails.
- synckit/converge/converge_test.go:287 (TestTombstonePersistedNotReconciled): tombstone must persist so removal propagates.
- cookiesync internal/state/state.go:158 RemoveBrowser doc: tombstones "so the delete propagates and survives a sync with a host that never saw it."

## Codex-confirmed
- Q1: fix (1) Remove-no-op AND fix (2) immediate Added==0-phantom drop BOTH resurrect a legitimately-deleted item when merged with a stale peer still holding the live add.
- Q2: NO data-level way to distinguish a phantom-bug tombstone from a legitimate cross-host-delete tombstone — both are Entry{Added:0, Removed:t, Value:zero}.
- Q3: horizon compaction is a grace-period heuristic, NOT unconditionally safe — resurrects for any peer offline longer than the horizon.

## Decision: BLOCKED, surfaced to orchestrator with options
Both specified requirements are unsafe. Only safe cleanup is horizon-gated Compact (drop non-present entries whose Removed < now-horizon, phantoms treated identically), but the horizon length is an undecided product-policy tradeoff and it deviates from the literal plan. No code changed in the worktree.</body>
<parameter name="labels">cregistry, convergence, F6, cookiesync
